rationale.root_cause: It pinpoints the unsafe copy at program.c:11 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explains that a command-line argument longer than the 256-byte buffer overflows it, explicitly calling it a global-buffer-overflow. This satisfies the requirement to localize the copy and explain argv can exceed the destination.
rationale.local_fix: It replaces the unbounded copy with a length-checked path: rejects inputs where strlen(argv[1]) >= sizeof buf, then copies, ensuring null termination. This meets the size check, rejection of overlong input, and preservation of NUL termination.
rationale.global_fix: It proposes removing raw unbounded string handling by introducing a bounded wrapper API that always takes destination size and banning unsafe APIs like strcpy. This addresses the root cause by enforcing bounded operations across the codebase.
